column3: DETECTION LOGIC (HAR JSON)
High count of sequential requests to the same base URL with incrementing IDs or params (e.g., /api/item/1, /api/item/2 …). Threshold: > 10 requests within 500ms.
Identical GET requests repeating at a fixed interval (e.g., every 2–5 s) with no user interaction. Detected by matching request.url + method across > 20 entries.
response.content.size > 1MB AND Content-Encoding header is absent on text/json/html responses. Compressible payload being sent raw.
Ratio of OPTIONS requests to GET/POST requests is > 1:1. Indicates missing Access-Control-Max-Age or credentials on every XHR.
Status 200 OK but response.content.size is 0 or {} repeatedly. Endpoint alive but returning empty payload — likely dead feature or misconfigured flag.
Same request.url appearing 3+ times with 301/302 status codes, forming a cycle. Loop depth > 3 triggers alert.
request.url domain ≠ App domain accounts for > 60% of total request count or > 50% of total transferred bytes.
Cache-Control: no-cache or no-store on static assets (.js, .css, .woff2, .png). These files should be fingerprinted and cached long-term.
Sensitive keys (e.g., token, set-cookie, authorization) found in unencrypted request headers over HTTP (not HTTPS). Protocol mismatch detected.
response.content.mimeType is text/html AND size > 500KB. Likely server-side rendering full page data instead of incremental/streaming.
timings.wait > 2000ms for more than 5% of requests to the same endpoint. P95 latency spike indicating backend bottleneck.
Identical method + url + request body sent more than once within 1s. Likely missing deduplication in client-side fetch logic.
Requests to /api/* endpoints without Authorization or Cookie headers returning 401/403. Unauthenticated calls leaking to network.
response.content.mimeType is application/json AND size > 2MB. API returning full dataset instead of paginated response.
Frequent HTTP polling requests alongside an open WebSocket connection. Client not properly routing to WS, falling back to REST.
request for .js resource with no async/defer attribute blocks HTML parsing. Detected via timings.blocked > 200ms on script entries.
Multiple requests for the same font file (woff2/woff) with different query strings. Indicates cache-busting on immutable fonts.
GraphQL POST body contains query selecting > 50 fields while response only uses < 10 of them based on payload key count heuristic.
Total size of Cookie request headers exceeds 4KB for non-SSO endpoints. Large cookie jar slowing every request.
Resources marked with rel=prefetch appear in HAR as full-latency first-time loads, suggesting prefetch hints are not being honored.
HTTP request fired from an HTTPS page. protocol mismatch detected in request.url while page URL is https://.
request.url contains query params matching pattern token=, api_key=, access_token=. Credentials exposed in URL and server logs.
Requests to cacheable assets (js/css/images) returning from network with no SW intercept header. Service worker not installed or skipped.
timings.dns > 300ms on any request. Indicates missing DNS prefetch or TTL too short on authoritative records.
timings.ssl > 500ms on HTTPS requests. Likely missing TLS session resumption or OCSP stapling not configured.
Same endpoint called 3+ times in < 2s with status 5xx. Client retry logic has no exponential back-off or jitter.
response.headers Cache-Control max-age is 0 or Expires is in the past for static fingerprinted asset. Resource always revalidated.
Transferred image size > 500KB and response Content-Type is image/*. No WebP/AVIF conversion or responsive srcset in use.
Requests to /v1/* and /v2/* of the same resource path coexist. Dual-version traffic suggests stale client bundle still live.
timings.blocked > 1000ms with no DNS/SSL/connect time. Pure queuing delay indicating browser connection limit hit (6 per origin).
Requests fired from unexpected origins (browser extensions or injected iframes) detected via initiator.url not matching app domain.
POST/PUT requests with Content-Length: 0 or missing body, returning 200 OK. Likely a fire-and-forget call with no payload validation.
Large video/audio resource (> 5MB) served without Accept-Ranges header. Cannot seek or stream; full download required.
Resource with stale-while-revalidate directive still blocking the render despite being within the revalidation window.
Single endpoint accounts for > 40% of total HAR request count. Single point of failure and bottleneck risk.
Token refresh endpoint (/auth/refresh or /oauth/token) called more than 5 times in 10s. Refresh token may be expiring prematurely.
response.content.size (compressed) is < 1KB but response.content.text decompressed size > 1MB. Potential decompression ratio attack.
HEAD requests sent to endpoints that return non-cacheable dynamic data. Wasted pre-flight with no caching benefit.
Transfer-Encoding: chunked response with timings.receive > 5000ms. Server streaming but chunks arriving with large gaps.
fetch() mode=no-cors responses detected (status 0). Cannot inspect content or errors; failure is silent.
Requests span > 10 unique domains in a single page load. Each unique origin adds DNS + TLS + connection overhead.
Critical above-fold resources (hero image, LCP element) loaded with low fetch priority while analytics scripts load at high priority.
Request for .css file returns 404. Stylesheet missing but still referenced in HTML, causing CSSOM build failure.
Request chain depth (A → B → C → …) exceeds 6 hops. Deeply nested dependency fetch chain inflating total load time.
image requests are not using responsive srcset or sizes attribute; same 2000px image served to 375px viewport.
timings.wait > 800ms on initial HTML document request. Backend TTFB above Core Web Vitals threshold.
User-Agent header in requests matches known bot/scraper patterns (Googlebot, HeadlessChrome, Python-requests) during dev/prod sessions.
request.headers contains X-Api-Key or x-functions-key with a hard-coded value consistent across all requests. Key rotation risk.
First HTTPS response missing Strict-Transport-Security header. Browser will not pin HTTPS for future visits.
navigator.sendBeacon POST requests firing with no associated user interaction or route change. Stale analytics event logic.
Performance-sensitive API endpoints return no Server-Timing header. Backend profiling data unavailable to frontend observability.
More than 6 simultaneous requests to the same origin observed. Requests beyond HTTP/1.1 limit are queued; upgrade to HTTP/2.
Response Content-Type is application/json but size > 5MB. Single large blob will block the main thread during JSON.parse().
Long-polling endpoint holds connection open > 30s. If connection drops, reconnect flood likely.
Conditional GET with If-None-Match sent even for non-cacheable dynamic endpoints. ETag revalidation overhead with no benefit.
webpack-hot-update.json or HMR WebSocket connections detected in production HAR. Dev tooling left enabled in prod bundle.
request.headers Origin does not match the actual page URL. Potentially forged origin or proxy misconfiguration.
SVG served as separate HTTP request > 200KB. Should be inlined or optimized with SVGO to reduce request + size overhead.
More than 10 consecutive 401 responses followed by no successful retry. Token refresh failed silently; user stuck in auth loop.
Referrer-Policy header absent; full URL including path and query string sent in Referer header to third-party domains.
Same dataset fetched by two sibling components independently. Detected by identical request.url with overlapping timings.
POST/PUT request with body but no Content-Type header. Server may reject or misparse the payload.
API response sets more than 10 Set-Cookie headers per response. Cookie jar bloat affecting all subsequent requests.
<link rel=preconnect> hints present in HTML but no requests to those origins appear within 2s. Wasted early connection.
Redirect chain longer than 3 hops before reaching final destination (2xx). Each hop adds a full round-trip latency.
Authorization: Bearer token with exp claim in the past is still being sent. Server likely returns 401; client not refreshing.
Requests to logging/telemetry endpoints firing on every user interaction (click, keydown). Over-instrumentation saturating log pipeline.
API response uses Cache-Control: public on endpoint returning user-specific data. Response may be cached by CDN and served to wrong user.
Requests with callback= query parameter detected. Legacy JSONP pattern is a XSS risk and should be replaced with CORS.
More than 3 distinct analytics/pixel domains (GA, GTM, FB Pixel, Hotjar, etc.) firing per page load with overlapping event data.
Single API endpoint returns > 1000 items in one response. No page or limit query param detected in request URL.
<link rel=preload> resource is fetched but not used within the page (detected by no matching render-blocking usage in HAR).
Two identical POST requests with the same body fired within 500ms. Double-submit bug from missing disabled state on submit button.
Third-party .js resource with timings.wait > 1500ms. Render-blocking or parser-blocking external script on critical path.
response.headers missing X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff on API responses. Browser may sniff and execute response as different MIME type.
Repeated tiny API calls (< 1KB each) that mirror localStorage read pattern. Data should be client-cached, not re-fetched on each access.
response.content.mimeType is text/html but request expected application/json (based on Accept header). Error page returned instead of JSON error.
Standard deviation of timings.wait for a single endpoint > 1000ms across multiple calls. Unpredictable backend performance.
CDN-hosted JS/CSS resources loaded without integrity attribute. No protection against CDN-level supply chain attacks.
Static assets (hashed filenames) being served with old hash post-deploy. CDN not invalidated; stale bundle in production.
request.url query string length > 2000 characters. Risk of 414 URI Too Long error on some proxies; data should be in POST body.
Connection: close header present on repeated requests to the same origin. Each request opens a new TCP connection.
PUT/POST with request.bodySize > 5MB and no progress tracking or chunked upload. Single failure aborts entire transfer.
Requests originating from a sandboxed cross-origin iframe to the parent app's API. CSRF risk if SameSite cookies not enforced.
Navigation-critical API called only after route transition. Resource not prefetched despite predictable navigation intent.
request.headers Accept contains invalid MIME types (e.g., */* mixed with explicit types improperly). May cause content negotiation failure.
XMLHttpRequest with async=false detected. Blocks main thread for entire duration of request.
Repeated DNS lookups for the same hostname (< 60s TTL inferred from repeated dns timings > 0). Excessive DNS resolution overhead.
Burst of API requests triggered by scroll events without debouncing. Detected by > 10 requests within 200ms on scroll-related endpoints.
Both native fetch and XMLHttpRequest to same endpoint in same session. Polyfill and native API active simultaneously.
PUT/PATCH responses missing ETag header. Clients cannot detect mid-air collisions on concurrent edits.
Large number of POST requests to /csp-report or report-uri endpoint. Page is generating many CSP violations in production.
WebSocket connection open but no messages exchanged for > 60s. Idle connection consuming server slot; missing heartbeat.
response.headers contains Deprecation or Sunset header signaling endpoint retirement, but requests continue normally.
First request to serverless endpoint has timings.wait > 3000ms followed by < 200ms for subsequent calls. Cold start not mitigated.
Multipart form-data upload split into many tiny parts (< 10KB each). Excessive part count adds overhead vs. fewer larger chunks.
Pre-signed S3/GCS URLs in request.url contain X-Amz-Expires or X-Goog-Expires with a value < 60s. Short-lived URLs causing fetch failures.
Client sends Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate without br. Brotli compression not offered, missing 15–20% size reduction vs. gzip.
callback= parameter value is not sanitized; server reflects arbitrary function name in response body. Scriptable response injection.
Client re-fetches data immediately after SSR page load (< 500ms). SSR hydration state not passed to client; double data load on every visit.
